entries.description: Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, session-set-authoring-guide.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed router restricted to end-of-session verification only this session and to use Gemini Pro as the verifier.
Wrote session-state.json marking session 1 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=1, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high, totalSessions=4).
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped; consistent with sessions 1-5 of set 001 and sessions 1-4 of set 002.
Confirmed Set 1 deliverables (queue_db.py, disposition.py, session_events.py) and Set 2 deliverables (mode-aware route()/verify(), enqueue path, role daemons) are present and importable.

Wrote ai-router/close_session.py: full CLI with all 9 documented flags (--session-set-dir, --json, --interactive, --force, --allow-empty-commit, --reason-file, --manual-verify, --repair, --apply, --timeout), CloseoutOutcome dataclass with stable JSON shape, exit-code table, idempotency via session-events.jsonl closed-state probe, disposition-required-or-force gate, closeout_requested/closeout_succeeded/closeout_failed event emission, gate-check skeleton with 5 named stub checks (working_tree_clean, pushed_to_remote, activity_log_entry, next_orchestrator_present, change_log_fresh), verification-wait skeleton (Session 3 fills polling), --repair skeleton walk (Session 4 fills detection logic).

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_skeleton.py with 22 unit tests covering: every documented flag accepted; --force/--interactive, --force/--manual-verify, --force/--repair, --apply-without-repair, and zero/negative timeout combinations rejected; missing session-set-dir → exit 2; missing disposition without --force → exit 2; --force bypass with no disposition → exit 0; happy-path skeleton → exit 0 with all 5 named gates passing; closeout_requested+closeout_succeeded events emitted and durable; idempotent no-op on already-closed set; --repair skeleton → exit 0; main() returns exit code; JSON output parseable; JSON shape stable on exit 2; RESULT_TO_EXIT_CODE table complete.
Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro (per user instruction: Gemini Pro). Cost $0.0283, in=13292t out=1172t. Saved raw output to session-reviews/session-001.md.

Round 1 verification by Gemini Pro returned ISSUES_FOUND with 3 issues (1 Major spec deviation, 2 Minor test gaps). All 3 fixed: --reason-file is now consumed and lands in closeout_requested event payload; closeout_failed emission test added via monkeypatch; missing-state fallback test added. Test count increased from 22 to 26; full suite 374 passed.
Routed round-2 verification to gemini-pro. Cost $0.0203, in=13780t out=310t. Appended raw output to session-reviews/session-001.md.

Read project-guidance.md, lessons-learned.md, ai-led-session-workflow.md, and spec.md. User instructed: no external provider routing this session except cross-provider verification routed to Gemini Pro.

Wrote session-state.json marking session 2 of set 003 in-progress (currentSession=2, lifecycleState=work_in_progress, orchestrator=claude-code/claude-opus-4-7@high). Appended work_started event to session-events.jsonl.
Per durable user instruction (memory: feedback_ai_router_usage), routed ai-assignment.md authoring intentionally skipped this session.

Created ai-router/gate_checks.py: 5 deterministic gate predicates with shared (passed, remediation) shape. check_working_tree_clean (scoped to session-set dir + files_changed allowlist + universal ignore patterns including .DS_Store/*.swp/.close_session.lock/session-events.jsonl, uses git status --porcelain -uall to defeat directory collapse). check_pushed_to_remote (enumerates detached HEAD, missing upstream, ahead-of-upstream, non-fast-forward, branch protection via git push --dry-run signal matching). check_activity_log_entry (verifies entry for currentSession exists). check_next_orchestrator_present (final-session shortcut; otherwise routes through validate_next_orchestrator). check_change_log_fresh (final-session-only; passes via mtime>=startedAt OR session-number reference in body). GATE_CHECKS registry preserves skeleton order.

Created ai-router/close_lock.py: file-based concurrency lock at <set>/.close_session.lock. acquire_lock uses O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomic create. Stale-lock reclaim via TTL (10 min) OR dead-PID detection. Cross-platform _pid_running: POSIX os.kill(pid, 0); Windows OpenProcess via ctypes with proper argtypes/restype and use_last_error=True for ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER vs ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED disambiguation. release_lock is no-op when foreign PID holds the file. close_session_lock context manager.

Replaced _run_gate_checks stub in close_session.py to iterate GATE_CHECKS registry; predicate exceptions become failed-gate remediation rather than crashing the flow. Wrapped run() main path and repair branch in acquire_lock/release_lock; lock contention surfaces result=lock_contention exit code 3. Lock reclaim warnings flow into outcome.messages.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_gate_checks.py: 24 tests. Helpers create real git repos with bare remotes. Coverage: clean tree pass, untracked-in-scope fail, universal pattern ignore (.DS_Store/.swp), out-of-scope filtering, not-a-git-repo fail; clean push pass, missing upstream, detached HEAD, ahead-of-upstream needs-push, divergent non-fast-forward; activity log present, missing state, missing log, no-matching-session; next_orchestrator final-skip, missing on non-final, malformed specifics, valid; change-log nonfinal-skip, missing on final, fresh-by-mtime, fresh-by-content-reference, stale-fail; registry order stability.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_lock.py: 10 tests. Acquire creates file with PID/worker_id/timestamp, release removes file. Second acquire raises LockContention. Stale by TTL reclaimed with warning. Stale by dead PID reclaimed with warning. Foreign-PID release is no-op (does not remove peer file). Already-gone release is no-op. Context manager acquires/releases on success and on exception; propagates LockContention.

Wrote ai-router/tests/test_close_session_integration.py: 7 end-to-end tests against real git repo + real session-set fixtures. Real close-out succeeds. Concurrent peer triggers lock_contention exit 3. Stale lock from dead PID is reclaimed; close-out proceeds with warning in messages. Uncommitted in-scope file triggers gate_failed exit 1 naming working_tree_clean. Final session without change-log triggers gate_failed naming change_log_fresh. --force still bypasses gates but still acquires the lock; --force is blocked by a live peer.

Updated test_close_session_skeleton.py to monkeypatch _run_gate_checks in the started_with_disposition fixture and the session-zero test, since the skeleton tests were previously written against a stub-passes-everything implementation. Skeleton tests now isolate flow-control concerns from gate-check correctness; gate correctness lives in test_gate_checks.py.
Full pytest suite: 415 passed in ~30s on Windows (was 374 in Session 1 baseline; +41 net new tests).

Routed end-of-session verification to gemini-pro per user instruction (overrode session-verification task_type_override which defaults to gpt-5-4). Round 1: Gemini Pro returned substantive pass with all 6 acceptance criteria affirmed Met, but used non-standard verdict wording ("**Verdict:** pass") that the orchestrator-side parser does not recognize. Cost $0.0519, in=34416t out=888t. Saved to session-reviews/session-002.md.

Round 2 re-verify with explicit schema instruction. Router auto-escalated past Gemini to Opus because Gemini response was short (21 tokens output) and tripped the truncation/short-response escalation heuristic. Round 2 verdict: VERIFIED (single line + summary, no issues). Escalation surfaced an unintended Anthropic spend ($0.5434) on top of the user instruction restricting routing this session — flagging transparently. Cost $0.5434, in=36124t out=21t. Appended to session-reviews/session-002.md.

Added lessons-learned entry: "Schema-Only Re-Verifies Need max_tier Pinned To Block Auto-Escalation". Captures the round-2 re-verify failure mode where a wording-only re-verify against Gemini Pro escalated to Opus due to short-response heuristic, costing an unintended Anthropic call. Action: future schema-only re-verifies must pass max_tier=<verifier_tier>.
Marked session 2 complete in session-state.json with verdict=VERIFIED. Emitted verification_completed and work_verified events.
